evals.prompt: Engagement: client wants to take a §199A position relying on a 2020 PLR addressing similar facts but with no on-point Tax Court or Court of Appeals authority. Confidence band has been assigned MODERATE (~ 40%). Disclosure on Form 8275? SSTS § 1.1 / Circular 230 § 10.37 implications?
evals.expected_output: Walks compliance checklist. SSTS § 1.1: MODERATE band (~ 40%) meets substantial-authority standard (≥ 33-40% probability), so position satisfies SSTS § 1.1 without disclosure under § 1.1(a). Note however: PLR is `written_determinations` weight; per shared/confidence-bands.md decay rule, drop one band when PLR is closest authority, so band might be LOW reasonable-basis under that rule. If LOW, disclosure required. Recommend: (1) confirm band assignment (PLR-only authority typically caps at MODERATE then drops to LOW under decay rule); (2) Form 8275 disclosure for LOW/reasonable-basis path; (3) document the substantial-authority analysis if MODERATE retained. Circular 230 § 10.37: written advice must reasonably consider all relevant facts and authorities; a PLR-only basis without searching for Tax Court / Court of Appeals authority may not satisfy § 10.37(a)(1)(iii) reasonable-effort prong. Recommend exhaustive primary-authority search before issuing written advice. Circular 230 §§ 10.22 / 10.35 / 10.37 satisfied subject to those steps.
expectations: Walks SSTS § 1.1 substantial-authority vs reasonable-basis distinction
Applies confidence-band decay rule for PLR-as-closest-authority
Recommends Form 8275 if band drops to LOW
JSON sidecar validates with verification_checklist populated

evals.prompt: Client has decided to set up a §831(b) micro-captive insurance company. The structure has Notice 2016-66 transaction-of-interest concerns. What disclosure obligations apply?
evals.expected_output: Walks compliance checklist. CRITICAL: §831(b) micro-captives are designated transactions of interest under Notice 2016-66 (and listed-transaction status pending in some IRS guidance — verify current). Form 8886 disclosure required (§6011 reportable-transaction). §6707A non-disclosure penalty: 75% of decrease in tax shown on return; max $200K per transaction; min $10K. Substantial economic-substance concerns under §7701(o); §6662(b)(6) 20% / (i) 40% strict-liability accuracy penalty for ESD-failed transactions; §6664(c)(2) bars reasonable-cause defense for §6662(b)(6). SSTS § 1.1: practitioner cannot recommend a SPECULATIVE-band position; this is recommend-do-not-proceed territory. Circular 230 § 10.37(a)(2): no audit-lottery considerations. Recommend: (1) decline engagement OR engage with extensive disclosure; (2) Form 8886 disclosure; (3) Form 8275 disclosure to avoid §6662(i) 40% rate; (4) document SSTS §1.1 / Circ 230 §10.37 advice that the practitioner advised against.
expectations: JSON sidecar validates with disclosure_forms array

evals.prompt: Client requests a written tax-advice memo concluding that their proposed structure produces a substantial federal tax benefit. What Circular 230 § 10.37 standards apply?
evals.expected_output:
Walks Circular 230 § 10.37 reasonable-practitioner standard. Six elements:
1. Reasonable factual and legal assumptions used.
2. All relevant facts and circumstances reasonably considered.
3. Reasonable efforts made to identify and ascertain facts.
4. No unreasonable reliance on representations / projections / appraisals.
5. Applicable law and authorities related to the facts.
6. No 'audit lottery' considerations.

Document each element in the memo's audit-defense file. Cite Circular 230 § 10.37, AICPA SSTS § 1.1. Recommend internal peer review before delivery; SSTS § 1.1(a)(2) practitioner-judgment items often benefit from co-counsel input. For high-stakes positions, include the negative-treatment-review residual responsibility for the practitioner.
expectations: JSON sidecar validates with verification_checklist populated
